title: Turkish Market Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **Days and hours: Tuesday and Friday, 11:00 to 18:30.** The Turkish Market runs along the Maybachufer in Neukölln, right beside the Landwehrkanal. It is closed the rest of the week.
**It is a Neukölln market, not Kreuzberg.** The stalls line the Neukölln bank; the leafy side across the canal is Kreuzberg. People call it "Kreuzberg’s Turkish market" out of habit.
**Nearest U-Bahn is Schönleinstraße (U8),** about a 5-minute walk. Kottbusser Tor (U8) and Hermannplatz (U7/U8) also work.
**Bring cash.** Most produce and street-food stalls are cash-only, and small notes and coins make everything faster.
**Timing changes the market.** Late morning is calm and best for produce; Friday afternoon is crowded and lively; the last half hour brings cheaper end-of-day produce.
**Saturday is a different market:** the Stoffmarkt (fabric market), 11:00 to 17:00, good for textiles and sewing supplies rather than food.
